Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a process tree for a Procure to Pay process that is underfitting The current model has 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Then a parallel block AND with Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice 4 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The issue is that the parallel block allows these activities to occur in any order and even concurrently which is not correct For example we cannot pay the invoice before receiving goods and the invoice and we cannot match the invoice to the PO before having both the PO and the invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The intended order is 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Create Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods 5 Receive Invoice 6 Match Invoice to PO which requires both the PO and the invoice 7 Pay Invoice which requires the invoice to be matched 8 Close Case

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: However note that steps 4 receive goods and 5 receive invoice are not strictly ordered with respect to each other In some processes the invoice might arrive before the goods

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: However in the standard Procure to Pay the matching is often three way PO invoice and goods receipt But the problem does not specify Since the activity is named Match Invoice to PO we assume it only requires 3 and 5

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given the list is numbered it implies a sequence So the intended sequence is exactly 1 to 8 in that order Therefore we must enforce that sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the fix is to replace the parallel block with a sequence of these five activities

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If we enforce the sequence 3 4 5 6 7 then it is satisfied

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The corrected process tree should have a sequence of all eight activities

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The entire process is now a strict sequence so each activity must occur in the fixed order This prevents any activity from occurring out of order such as paying the invoice before receiving it or matching before having the invoice and PO
